steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage structures three separate viewpoints — from mother Haesu, father Chun, and daughter Faye — as "The first section... the second... the last," emphasizing they are different perspectives.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank must mean "clearly separate or different" — each family member's section is its own viewpoint, not overlapping. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — required: necessary or obligatory — this implies the perspectives are mandated somehow, not that they differ from one another. ✗
  • Choice B — unknown: unfamiliar or unidentified — the perspectives are explicitly identified (Haesu, Chun, Faye), so calling them unknown contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — unintended: accidental or not planned — this suggests Kim stumbled into three viewpoints, contradicting the deliberate creative structure described. ✗
  • Choice D — distinct: clearly different and separate — each section has its own named narrator, making the perspectives genuinely distinct from one another. ✓
D is correct.
